layer_1_flower.intent: Electric cerebral joy with diesel fuel clarity

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Black Pepper carries the dominant beta-caryophyllene. Lemon matches the diesel aroma note. Sage provides α-Humulene support. Lemongrass rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A high-energy session. Electric cerebral joy with diesel fuel clarity. Genres: funk, afrobeat, disco, nu-jazz. Tempo around 100-128 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Sour Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Diesel and black pepper infused green tea blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 175°F.
Add green tea tea bag and steep for 3-4 minutes.
Add charcoal-roasted hojicha and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add cracked black peppercorn and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Sour Fizz
method: Fill a highball glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz charred rosemary sprig and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz cracked black pepper.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with diesel.

cocktail.name: Sour Collins
cocktail.description: Blanco Tequila-based diesel and black pepper cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz blanco tequila, 1.5 oz charred rosemary sprig, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz cracked black pepper.
Add ice.
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled highball.
Garnish with diesel.

juice.name: Sour Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh apple through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Sour Shot
ingredients: tiny pinch of hint of lemon
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz tequila with 0.75 oz fresh apple juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Edamame with Chili Flakes & Sea Salt
snack.desc: Steamed edamame tossed with chili oil, flaky salt, and toasted sesame.

appetizer.name: Spring Rolls with Peanut Sauce
appetizer.desc: Fresh rice paper rolls with herbs, vermicelli, and spicy peanut dipping sauce.

main.name: Thai Green Curry with Fresh Vegetables
main.desc: Coconut-based green curry with seasonal vegetables, Thai basil, and jasmine rice.

dessert.name: Key Lime Pie Bars
dessert.desc: Tangy key lime curd on graham cracker crust with whipped cream.

cheese.name: Queso Fresco & Grilled Fruit
cheese.desc: Crumbled queso fresco with grilled peaches, honey, and pepitas.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your electric space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Open space, natural light when possible. Standing or high-top surfaces. Plants visible.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 20 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin 5 minutes before the session. Maintain rhythm throughout opening track.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Sit tall. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, feeling energy rise through the chest. Brief hold for two. Exhale through the mouth for four counts with gentle force, like stoking a flame.

layer_13_narrative.text:
15 drops of black pepper carry the dominant β-Caryophyllene through the room brightly — the way Alpenglow Farms intended when they cultivated Sour Diesel in Humboldt County. The lineage reads Sour Diesel BX4 (Garden of Grease), but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: diesel, black pepper, and hint of lemon.

The second oil — Lemon — adds 14 drops of brightness that matches the black pepper note in the flower itself. Cerebral is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body awakens into energetic with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.27% total can deliver.

In the sun-filled space, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of β-Caryophyllene, already primed for electric cerebral joy with diesel fuel clarity. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Alpenglow Farms grows in Humboldt County: not just cannabis, but context. The sativa genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as bay laurel on a Humboldt hillside. The session awakens into its final form — joyful, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Rotundone in Syrah shares the spicy, peppery character of β-caryophyllene — same aromatic family, different kingdoms.
wine.tasting_note: Dark fruit, cracked pepper, coastal minerality

wine.terpene_bridge: Zinfandel's brambly spice and warm pepper notes echo caryophyllene's warming, anti-inflammatory character.

beer.terpene_bridge: Belgian yeast esters produce spicy, peppery phenolics that echo caryophyllene's warming quality.
Centennial hops carry significant caryophyllene — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
